articles.title: Azerbaijan Sold Gold Worth $3 Billion in First Ever Drawdown

articles.title: DOJ Drops Powell Probe, Smoothing Path for Warsh to Fed
articles.summary: The Justice Department is ending a controversial investigation into building-renovation cost overruns by the Federal Reserve, potentially clearing a path to confirmation for Kevin Warsh, President Donald Trump’s pick to be the next Fed leader. articles.title: Pakistan Expects to Host Another Round of US-Iran Talks
articles.summary: Pakistani officials expect to host another round of peace talks between Iran and the US. Iran's top diplomat is going there, but it's not clear if a US delegation is as well. articles.title: Pakistan Buys LNG Supply From Spot Market to Ease Energy Crunch

articles.title: DOJ Expected to Drop Powell Investigation: ABC

articles.title: Finma Says Immediate Mythos Access Would Pose Systemic Bank Risk

articles.title: Inflation Fears Drive Consumer Sentiment to Record Low

articles.title: Yara Boosts Profit as Iran War Sends Fertilizer Prices Soaring
articles.summary: Fertilizer giant Yara International ASA posted higher-than-expected first quarter earnings as the Iran war effectively halted transit through the Strait of Hormuz, hurting global trade of crop nutrients and pushing up prices.

articles.title: Nomura Posts Record Full-Year Profit on Japan Market Rebound
articles.summary: Nomura Holdings Inc. cemented a second straight year of record profit, fueled by Japan’s financial-market recovery, even after fourth-quarter results missed analysts’ expectations.

articles.summary: China saw strong demand at its first ultra-long special bond sale of the year, with 30-year yields reaching the lowest level since a November auction.

articles.title: China Scaled Back Fiscal Stimulus in First Month of War in Iran
articles.summary: China pulled back on fiscal spending in March as the economy rebounded at the start of the year despite disruptions caused by the war in Iran.

articles.title: One by One, Europe’s Safest Government Bonds Fall From Grace
articles.summary: The traditional hierarchy of debt in Europe is facing its latest shake-up as worsening public finances in Belgium threaten to turn some of the region’s once-safest bonds into a risky bet.

articles.title: Japan Expert Sees Risk to the Yen
articles.summary: Former BOJ Board member Sayuri Shirai and BlackRock Japan Managing Director and Portfolio Manager Rie Shigekawa speak with Bloomberg reporter Lisa Du at the Bloomberg New Voices 2026 event in Tokyo. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: ADP Is Said Planning to Reduce Stake in India’s GMR Airports
articles.summary: ADP, operator of the main Paris airports, is planning to reduce its stake in India’s GMR Airports Ltd., people familiar with the matter said.

articles.title: Siemens Energy Raises Outlook on Strong AI-Driven Demand
articles.summary: Gas-turbine and energy-equipment maker Siemens Energy AG raised its outlook for the fiscal year 2026, citing strong demand.

articles.title: Oil Trader Andurand Lost 52% in April First Half on Oil Bets
articles.summary: Energy trader Pierre Andurand’s largest hedge fund plunged about 52% in the first half of April, wiping out first quarter gains made on bullish oil bets at the start of the Iran war.

articles.title: US Stocks Waver on Mixed Earnings and US-Iran Impasse
articles.summary: US stocks swung between narrow gains and losses on Thursday after Israel said it was poised to resume the war with Iran, while the impasse between Washington and Tehran continued.

articles.title: Pimco Privately Lends $10 Billion to Gulf in Wartime Deals
articles.summary: As Persian Gulf states build cash buffers to deal with any potential economic fallout from the Iran war, one large buyer has stepped in: Pacific Investment Management Co.

articles.title: Sheinbaum Points to Local Officials After CIA Agent Deaths
articles.summary: Mexico’s President Claudia Sheinbaum sought to ease tensions with the US after the death of CIA agents following a recent drug lab bust in northern Mexico, blaming an opposition state government for failing to adhere to proper security protocols.

articles.title: Rogers’ Spending Cut Plan Prompts Two Analysts to Boost Ratings
articles.summary: Rogers Communications Inc.’s decision to pull back on capital spending and boost free cash flow this year prompted at least two analysts to raise their recommendation on the telecommunications firm after the company released its first-quarter results Wednesday.

articles.title: Dow CEO Says Petrochemical Supply Disruptions to Persist in 2026
articles.summary: Dow Inc., one of the world’s largest producers of petrochemicals, expects supply disruptions tied to the war in Iran to persist throughout the remainder of 2026, according to Chief Executive Officer Jim Fitterling.

articles.title: Uruguay Finance Chief Says Pensions Will Stay in Private Hands
articles.summary: Uruguay’s pension savings will continue to be managed by the private sector under the government’s planned overhaul of the social security system, Finance Minister Gabriel Oddone said.

articles.title: Treasuries Headed for Tightest Monthly Trading Range Since 2020
articles.summary: US Treasury benchmark yields have been locked in the narrowest range since the depths of the coronavirus pandemic, as investors fatigued by conflicting geopolitical headlines search for direction.

articles.title: CAAT Pension Earns 8.4% as Stock Gains Outweigh Weak PE Returns
articles.summary: CAAT Pension Plan earned 8.4% last year as buoyant stock performance outweighed soft returns in the fund’s private market portfolio.

articles.title: Gold Falls as US-Iran Hormuz Standoff Heightens Inflation Risk
articles.summary: Gold declined as an escalation in tension over the Strait of Hormuz drove oil prices higher, rekindling concerns over high inflation.

articles.title: The WNBA's Expansion into Cleveland is Powered by PE
articles.summary: In this episode of The Deal, Kara Nortman breaks down for Alex Rodriguez and Jason Kelly how Monarch, her private equity firm, became the first PE firm approved to invest in the WNBA and why they chose Cleveland as their team (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Bloomberg Surveillance 4/23/2026
articles.summary: Jonathan Ferro, Lisa Abramowicz and Annmarie Hordern speak daily with leaders and decision makers from Wall Street to Washington and beyond. No other program better positions investors and executives for the trading day. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Avis Shares Plunge 62% in Two Days in Crashing Halt to Rally
articles.summary: A searing surge in the shares of Avis Budget Group Inc. has hit a wall, with the stock plunging over 62% in just two days.

articles.title: Czech Utility Giant CEZ Plans Asset Split Before Possible Buyout
articles.summary: Czech power utility CEZ AS outlined a plan to split its assets, a move that could pave the way for the government to obtain full ownership of its electricity production in a long-planned buyout.

articles.title: Why EV Demand Is Stronger Than It Looks
articles.summary: Tesla is tripling AI spending to $25B as its robotics ambitions ramp up, while EV sales send mixed signals. TD Cowen’s Itay Michaeli joined Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why demand may be stronger than it looks, why losing tax credits hurt more than high gas prices helped, and how upcoming EV l

articles.title: New Mountain Raises $2.4 Billion to Hold Azuria Water Longer
articles.summary: New Mountain Capital collected $2.4 billion to extend its hold on infrastructure firm Azuria Water Solutions, underscoring the booming market for secondhand private equity stakes.

articles.title: American Express Sees Strong Card Spending, Higher Costs
articles.summary: American Express Co. plans to boost spending on marketing and technology and said its customers’ spending on air travel has been slowing. Romaine Bostick discusses his interview with American Express CEO Stephen Squeri on "Bloomberg Open Interest." (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: The Deal with Alex Rodriguez and Jason Kelly | Season 4A Trailer
articles.summary: The Deal with Alex Rodriguez and Jason Kelly showcases intimate conversations with business titans and sports champions that reveal their investment philosophies, missed opportunities and more. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Hasbro Preliminary Sales Beat Estimates, Buoyed by ‘Magic’
articles.summary: Hasbro Inc. reported preliminary revenue that beat analysts’ estimates, buoyed by its fantasy card game Magic: The Gathering. The toy company said it couldn’t release full quarterly results at this time due to a previously disclosed cybersecurity attack.

articles.title: Teck Warns of Higher Fuel Costs From Mideast Energy Shock
articles.summary: Canada’s Teck Resources Ltd. warned of higher fuel costs for its key Chilean copper mines as the war in the Middle East chokes supply chains, even as higher commodity prices buoyed the company’s earnings in the first quarter.

articles.title: AI Junk-Bond Binge Brings Rare Early Repayments to Sweeten Deals
articles.summary: As a more than $20 billion borrowing frenzy to build out data centers descended on the junk-bond market this year, some issuers offered up a rare sweetener: an early cash payback.

articles.title: American Airlines Lowers Outlook on Rising Fuel Costs
articles.summary: American Airlines announced it expects $4 billion in additional expenses due to rising jet fuel prices but plans to raise fares to offset these costs, maintaining some of the price increases even if fuel prices return to prewar levels. Siddharth Philip has more on "Bloomberg Open Interest." (Source:

articles.title: India Contracts Fertilizer at Nearly Double Pre-War Price
articles.summary: India, the world’s top importer of urea, has booked the nitrogen-based fertilizer at sharply higher prices than in a previous tender as the Middle East conflict disrupts supplies and pushes global benchmarks higher.

articles.title: Sainsbury’s Says Middle East Conflict Risks Weighing on Profit
articles.summary: J Sainsbury Plc said profit could slip this year as Britain’s second-largest grocer faces higher costs and consumer uncertainty triggered by the conflict in the Middle East.

articles.title: Yuan to Surpass Yen in Currency Options Trading, LCH Says
articles.summary: The yuan is on track to overtake the yen as the second-most traded currency against the dollar in the foreign-exchange options market, according to London-based clearing house LCH.

articles.title: Nestlé Sales Rise as Snacks and Coffee Outweigh Recall Hit
articles.summary: Nestlé SA showed resilient sales growth in the first quarter, helping the Swiss foodmaker contain the fallout from the largest product recall in its history.

articles.title: Stock Futures Fall, Pound Weakens on Talks Uncertainty
Nokia Earnings Beat Estimates as AI Pivot Shows Promise
articles.summary: Nokia Oyj reported first-quarter adjusted profit that beat analyst forecasts, as the Finnish mobile network equipment maker’s push into artificial intelligence and cloud infrastructure begins to pay off.

articles.title: SET President: IPO Pipeline Faces a Difficult Situation
articles.summary: Stock Exchange of Thailand President Asadej Kongsiri discussed the IPO pipeline, saying it is in a difficult situation. He also noted that the Thai exchange plans to introduce “mini-gold” futures. He spoke with Haslinda Amin on Bloomberg Insight. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Indonesia Rupiah Falls Most in Seven Months on Rising Oil Prices
articles.summary: The Indonesian rupiah declined by the most in seven months as rising oil prices hurt sentiment for the nation’s assets, as Bank Indonesia signal that they have increased intervention efforts.

articles.title: Modi Seeks to Win Key India State After Voter Rolls Purged
articles.summary: Just two weeks before voters in India’s eastern state of West Bengal were due to go to the polls, about 9 million names were removed from the registered roll, causing an uproar among opposition groups.

articles.title: Indonesia Sells Biggest Samurai Bond Since 2024 on Firm Demand
articles.summary: Indonesia raised ¥172.1 billion ($1.1 billion) in its biggest Samurai bond sale in two years as strong investor demand for its credit outweighed concerns about budgetary pressures due to the Middle East war.

articles.title: Hyundai Motor Misses Estimates as Geopolitical Headwinds Hit
articles.summary: Hyundai Motor Co. first-quarter earnings missed estimates, as South Korea’s largest automaker grappled with US tariffs and cooling demand in key markets.

articles.title: Roche First-Quarter Sales Drop, Hurt by Copycats and Currencies
articles.summary: Roche Holding AG reported declining sales for the first quarter, hurt by competition for its older medicines and the Swiss franc’s appreciation against the dollar.

articles.title: JPMorgan to Add Philippine Bonds to Key EM Index in 2027
articles.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. said it will add local-currency bonds from the Philippines into its widely followed emerging-market index early next year, a move that may draw foreign investment and help spur the nation’s economy.

articles.title: Big Bet on Orban’s Exit Came From Center of His Family’s Empire
articles.summary: An asset-management fund in the Equilor group of companies part-owned by Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s son-in-law profited from financial-market bets on the strongman losing power.

articles.title: How the Mideast War is Affecting the Trump-Xi Meeting

articles.title: Pakistan Rushes to Buy LNG From Spot Market to Ease Gas Shortage
articles.summary: Pakistan is looking to buy liquefied natural gas from the expensive spot market for the first time in over two years as the government rushes to ease an energy shortfall triggered by the conflict in the Middle East.

articles.title: Sinking TSMC ADR Premium Offers Trading Window, UBS Desk Says
articles.summary: The narrowing gap between Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.’s Taiwanese shares and its US-listed stock is creating a new trading opportunity, according to a UBS Group AG client note.

articles.title: Five Reasons Global Markets Are Holding Up Despite War in Iran
articles.summary: Nearly two months into the conflict in Iran, global stock markets are staging a defiant rally. From the US to Taiwan and South Korea, a disconnect has emerged: while the geopolitical tensions remain high, equities are charging back toward all-time highs.

articles.title: Singapore Airlines Deepens Role at Air India Amid Record Losses
articles.summary: Singapore Airlines Ltd. is deepening its operational involvement in Air India Ltd., as the carrier battles record losses and recent safety lapses draw increasing concern, according to people familiar with the matter.

articles.title: Trampled Underfoot by a Stampede of Unicorns
articles.summary: The valuations behind a string of planned IPOs could drain life from stock indexes.

articles.title: Hormuz Traffic Grinds to a Halt After Iran Seizes First Vessels

articles.title: Oil Holds Gains as Peace Negotiations Between US and Iran Stall

articles.title: Fair Isaac Shares Slump as Pulte Moves to Cut Credit Score Costs

articles.title: Bessent Says US Allies in Gulf, Asia Requested Swap Lines

articles.title: GE Vernova Surges After Quarterly Orders Topped All of Last Year

articles.title: Reckitt Sales Hit by Lackluster US Demand for Cold Medicines
articles.summary: Reckitt Benckiser Group Plc’s reported slower than expected sales due to a weak cold and flu season in the US and supply disruption in the Middle East.

articles.title: US IPO Candidates Discouraged By Risk of Litigation, Red Tape
articles.summary: The risk of being sued after an initial public offering is one of the biggest concerns for potential US listing candidates, according to more than 150 executives surveyed by Bloomberg Intelligence.

articles.summary: US equity-index futures climbed and oil dropped after President Donald Trump said he would extend the ceasefire with Iran, spurring cautious optimism among investors.

articles.summary: Gold regained some ground after a two-day decline, as US President Donald Trump extended a ceasefire with Iran and allowed more time to arrange fresh peace talks.

articles.title: Getty Images Downgraded on Weaker Liquidity, Merger Uncertainty
articles.summary: Moody’s Ratings cut Getty Images Inc.’s credit rating deeper into junk territory, citing weakening liquidity, with lingering uncertainty over a potential merger with Shutterstock Inc. adding to the pressure.

articles.title: PBOC Injects Cash Despite Flush Liquidity, Fueling Bond Rally
articles.summary: China’s central bank has injected more cash into the banking system, signaling unusual tolerance for abundant liquidity and boosting confidence that the bond rally may have further to run.

articles.title: Indonesia Bourse to Remove Tightly Held Firms From Some Indexes
articles.summary: The Indonesia Stock Exchange plans to remove firms identified for having high shareholding concentration from some of its key indexes as part of ongoing market reforms.

articles.title: Capital One Boosts Provision for Bad Loans, Misses Estimates
articles.summary: Capital One Financial Corp., the biggest US credit-card lender, reported a first-quarter profit that missed Wall Street estimates and set aside more cash to cover soured loans.

articles.title: Vanguard Scoops Up Treasuries as Iran Conflict Lifts Yields
articles.summary: Vanguard is boosting its holdings of Treasuries, taking advantage of higher yields following the Middle East conflict to lock in rates and hedge against the risks of a potential growth slowdown.

articles.title: Commodity Traders Reap Profit Bonanza as Iran War Upends Markets
articles.summary: The world’s biggest energy traders are reaping a fresh profit bonanza as the war in Iran causes unprecedented disruption to oil markets.

articles.title: Baird Capital Raises $450 Million for Private Equity Fund
articles.summary: The private equity arm of financial services firm Robert W. Baird & Co. has raised $450 million for a fund that will invest in lower middle market companies in the US and the UK.

articles.title: Switch Taps $2.6 Billion of Bank Cash for Data Center Push
articles.summary: Switch Inc. gathered $2.6 billion of bank pledges to help the data-center operator procure electricity, a mounting challenge for the booming industry and one of its biggest costs.

articles.title: Trump Extends Iran Ceasefire Until Talks Conclude
articles.summary: President Donald Trump posted on social media that he would extend the ceasefire with Iran, after plans for diplomatic talks between the two countries were scrapped. Joe Mathieu reports on "Bloomberg The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Bond Traders Bet on Post-War Calm to Keep Rates in Tight Range
articles.summary: Traders are building up bets that bond market volatility will keep falling, even as a US-Iran peace agreement remains elusive.

articles.title: Markets Extend Downturn | Closing Bell
articles.summary: Comprehensive cross-platform coverage of the U.S. market close on Bloomberg Television, Bloomberg Radio, and YouTube with Romaine Bostick, Isabelle Lee, Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Deutsche Telekom Weighs Full Combination With T-Mobile
articles.summary: Deutsche Telekom AG is considering a full combination with its American arm T-Mobile US Inc., a move that would create a multinational telecom group and rank as the largest-ever public M&A deal, people with knowledge of the matter said.

articles.title: Warsh Says Fed ‘Must Stay in Its Lane’
articles.summary: Bloomberg's Mike McKee detailed changes to the Federal Reserve that fed chair nominee Kevin Warsh revealed in his confirmation hearing Tuesday, saying that Warsh may want to change or eliminate economic guidance. Warsh told Senators that he is not a fan of the Fed's forward guidance and feels that i

articles.title: Paraguay Leaves Key Rate at 5.5% on Stable Inflation Outlook
articles.summary: Paraguay’s central bank held its benchmark interest rate unchanged for a second-consecutive month at 5.5% amid a stable inflation outlook.

articles.title: Top Oil Traders Say Billion-Barrel Shock to Echo Long After War
articles.summary: The impact of the Iran war will continue for months even after any deal to restore shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, the world’s largest oil traders have warned. Some said flows through the waterway may never return to normal.

articles.title: Terna CEO Ready to Waive Severance After Italy’s Meloni Demand
articles.summary: The outgoing chief executive officer of grid operator Terna SpA, Giuseppina di Foggia, is willing to waive a €7.3 million ($8.6 million) severance to take a new role as chairwoman of ENI SpA after Italian Premier Giorgia Meloni said she had to decide between the two options.

articles.title: Vance Trip to Pakistan for Talks With Iran Is On Hold, NYT Reports
articles.summary: The New York Times reports that Vice President JD Vance's trip to Pakistan for peace talks with Iran is on hold for now. We hear more from Republican strategist Lanhee Chen and Jeanne Sheehan Zaino, a Bloomberg contributor, on "Balance of Power." (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Warsh's Undisclosed Investments Raise Questions on Influence Says Richards
articles.summary: Former Treasury official Kitty Richards, senior strategic adviser at the Groundwork Collaborative, said the financial disclosures of Kevin Warsh, the nominee to be chair of the Federal Reserve, leave some important questions unanswered because some of his holdings remain under wraps. Richards also s

articles.title: Here comes the food price inflation
articles.summary: Input costs are already on the rise

articles.title: Senator Tillis Floats Off-Ramp to Move Warsh’s Fed Nomination Forward
articles.summary: Senator Thom Tillis, a Republican from North Carolina, says that he would be in favor of a joint committee investigation into the oversight of renovations at the Federal Reserve. Tyler Kendall reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Warsh Pledges Independence But Dodges Questions on Rates
articles.summary: Kevin Warsh repeatedly pledged to act independently if he’s confirmed as the next Federal Reserve chair, rejecting Democratic concerns he would be a “sock puppet” for President Donald Trump, who continues to demand lower interest rates. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Sen. Tillis Calls for End to Powell Probe During Warsh Hearing
articles.summary:
Republican Sen. Thom Tillis called on the Department of Justice to end its probe into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell for the Fed building cost overruns so he can support Kevin Warsh’s nomination.

articles.title: Tim Cook's Exit, Warsh Faces Congress | Open Interest 4/21/2026
articles.summary: Get a jump start on the US trading day with Matt Miller and Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest." Apple’s era shifts as Tim Cook steps down after 15 years, with John Ternus set to take over.  And Kevin Warsh faces Capitol Hill in a high-stakes Fed showdown. We'll take you to Capitol Hill. Plus,

articles.title: Here Are the Key Takeaways From Kevin Warsh’s Fed Confirmation Hearing
articles.summary: Here are the key takeaways from Kevin Warsh’s remarks at his confirmation hearing to be the next chairman of the Federal Reserve. He testified before the US Senate Banking Committee on Tuesday.

articles.title: Citadel Securities Takes On Banks With Push for Big Stock Trades
articles.summary: Jamie Dimon’s letter to JPMorgan Chase & Co. shareholders published this month runs to about 25,000 words. For Wall Street, two in particular stand out: “Citadel Securities.”

articles.title: Davidson Kempner Ex-Trader Says He Blew Whistle, Was Ousted
articles.summary: A former top trader and partner at Davidson Kempner Capital Management accused the firm and its investing chief, Tony Yoseloff, of ousting him last year after he attempted to expose wrongdoing.

articles.title: Oil Traders Warn of Recession Impact as Hormuz Hits Demand
articles.summary: The world’s top oil traders warned that the ongoing closure of the Strait of Hormuz is increasing the risk of a global recession as fuel demand takes a hit.

articles.title: US Says It Boarded Oil Tanker, First During Iranian Blockade
articles.summary: The US government said it stopped and boarded a sanctioned oil tanker in its campaign to disrupt Iran’s shipping network, the first such intervention since the imposition of a blockade just over a week ago.

articles.title: Mercuria Was Able to Get Ships Out Through Hormuz, CEO Says
articles.summary: Mercuria Energy Group Ltd. was reasonably well positioned before the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, and has been able to get ships out even after war erupted at the end of February, its co-founder and chief executive officer said.

articles.title: Hybrid Bond Buybacks Return After War Paused Riskier Deals
articles.summary: European companies are once again buying back junior bonds after the market turmoil sparked by the war in the Middle East and the AI-related software selloff put some riskier debt deals on ice.

articles.title: Wall Street’s Crypto Ambitions Dealt a Setback by Latest Hack
articles.summary: The weekend hack that drained nearly $300 million from a small crypto project and triggered a $10 billion run on the largest decentralized lending platform could slow Wall Street’s growing interest in blockchain technology, according to Jefferies LLC.

articles.title: Tax-the-Rich Boosts Appeal of Bonds That Give Shelter to Wealthy
articles.summary: Democrats around the country are pushing forward on plans to tax the rich — moves that are supportive for the tax-exempt municipal-bond market.

articles.title: European Stocks Drop on Geopolitical Angst; Royal Unibrew Sinks
articles.summary: European stocks fell on Tuesday as investors weighed a flurry of earnings and tracked developments in the Middle East conflict ahead of Wednesday’s ceasefire deadline.

articles.title: US Stocks Decline as Investors Mull Warsh Testimony, Iran
articles.summary: US stocks traded lower on Tuesday as investors pored over the latest corporate earnings while assessing the updates on the Iran ceasefire talks as well as Kevin Warsh’s Senate hearing.

articles.title: Crisis y sanciones de Trump limitan las opciones a cubanos que quieren salir de la isla
articles.summary: En medio de la campaña de Donald Trump para privar a Cuba de combustible y aislarla políticamente, los cubanos tienen cada vez menos opciones para salir de su isla gobernada por el comunismo. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Rising Costs Threaten the Airline Boom
articles.summary: Airlines are walking a tightrope: strong demand and pricing power on one side, rising fuel and labor costs on the other. articles.title: Canadian Telecom Rogers Goes Into Tailspin as Price War Heats Up
articles.summary: Rogers Communications Inc. has gone from the top performer among Canada’s three big telecommunications companies to the sector’s biggest drag, as a wireless price war threatens its premium valuation.

articles.title: MUFG Bank Seeks More Acquisitions After $4.3 Billion India Deal
articles.summary: MUFG Bank Ltd. is looking for opportunities to make further acquisitions or investments in Asia and elsewhere, according to the new head of the lending unit at Japan’s largest banking group.

articles.title: HORMUZ TRACKER: Traffic Stays at a Trickle of Iran-Aligned Ships
articles.summary: The Strait of Hormuz remained largely devoid of commercial traffic on Tuesday as Iran continues enforcing a renewed closure of the waterway, leaving only a trickle of small vessels passing through. Most have ties to Tehran but are staying local.

articles.title: Trump Urges Judge Not to Let Dimon ‘Escape’ JPMorgan Lawsuit
articles.summary: Donald Trump’s lawyer said JPMorgan Chase & Co. Chief Executive Officer Jamie Dimon can’t “escape liability” for allegedly blacklisting the president after Jan. 6, 2021.

articles.title: Brad Jacobs on His Big Bet on Building Insulation
articles.summary: The famed dealmaker on his latest acquisition.

articles.title: Tiny Iran-Linked Tanker Tests US Blockade With Arabian Sea Move
articles.summary: A tiny tanker that’s reportedly transporting liquefied gas from Iran is testing a US blockade of the Islamic Republic’s shipping.

articles.title: Odd Lots: QXO’s Brad Jacobs on His Big Insulation Bet (Podcast)
articles.summary: He’s done it again. On Sunday night, building supply company QXO announced that it would be acquiring TopBuild for $17 billion. TopBuild sells and installs insulation for both the residential and commercial markets. For Brad Jacobs, the CEO of QXO, this is just the latest in a lifetime of deals he’s

articles.title: Ugandan Banks Balk at New ‘Foreign Agents’ Registration Law
articles.summary: Ugandan banks are seeking an exemption from proposed legislation requiring all businesses and individuals that receive funds from outside the country to be registered, arguing that the measure will negatively impact their ability to raise capital.

articles.title: Ukraine Willing to Delay Access to EU Benefits, Deputy PM Kachka Says
articles.summary: Ukraine is open to delay access to the EU Common Agricultural Policy (CAP) funding initially, however, it may become part of negotiations over the next seven-year budget, which would begin in 2034, Ukraine’s Deputy Prime Minister Taras Kachka says in an interview on Bloomberg TV. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Le Pen’s Energy Plan for France Called a ‘Disaster’ by Engie CEO
articles.summary: A plan by Marine Le Pen’s far-right party to scrap renewables and the energy regulator would be a disaster for France, the CEO of giant utility Engie SA said, in one of the sharpest rebukes from a business leader ahead of next year’s presidential election.

articles.title: Riskiest Junk Debt Is Lagging as Investors Continue to Fret About Software Firms’ AI Future
articles.summary: Investors, worried about artificial intelligence disrupting software companies, are skirting the most-troubled US junk debt even as they embrace risk in other markets.

articles.title: US Natural Gas Ticks Down on Mild Weather, Growing Surplus
articles.summary: US natural gas futures fell slightly as weather forecasts shifted warmer, indicating slightly lower demand for the heating and power-plant fuel. Expectations that domestic stockpiles will move higher above the five-year average may also be putting pressure on prices.

articles.title: Saudi IT Firm to Press Ahead with IPO Despite War Uncertainty
articles.summary: Saudi Arabian information technology services firm Dar Al Balad Business Solutions Co. is pressing ahead with plans to list on the kingdom’s stock exchange despite the disruptions caused by the regional conflict.

articles.title: Treasuries Weaken Before Warsh Testifies for Fed Chair Role
articles.summary: Treasuries fell as strong economic data prompted traders to scale back rate-cut expectations ahead of testimony from Federal Reserve Chair nominee Kevin Warsh.

articles.title: BMO, Teck, TC Energy CEOs to Advise Carney on US Strategy on New Panel
articles.summary: Prime Minister Mark Carney is turning to some of Canada’s most prominent business executives, including Bank of Montreal’s Darryl White, to give him advice on how to handle Canada’s economic relationship with the US.

articles.title: German Pension VBL Shifts €7 Billion Housing Portfolio Into Fund
articles.summary: German public-sector pension fund Versorgungsanstalt des Bundes und der Länder, or VBL, is transferring its residential property portfolio worth around €7 billion ($8.2 billion) into a new fund structure.

articles.title: Billionaire Fredriksen’s Firm Faces $1 Billion Claim After Failed Fraud Trial
articles.summary: Two former executives at billionaire John Fredriksen’s oil trading firm sued for about $1 billion in over allegations that the shipping tycoon’s failed “vindictive” lawsuit brought them to the “brink of financial ruin.”

articles.title: Core Scientific Adds to AI Junk-Bond Wave With $3.3 Billion Deal
articles.summary: Core Scientific Inc. is looking to raise $3.3 billion from a junk-bond sale, joining a wave of high-yield issuers tapping debt markets to fund artificial intelligence infrastructure.

articles.title: Polish Arms Maker Set for Record Sales on Defense Spending Spree
articles.summary: Poland’s biggest defense group is targeting another record year as the European Union’s largest land force ramps up spending to counter threats from Russia and tap into new EU funding.

articles.title: [단독] "한국 공장 인근서 합숙"…엔비디아도 K부품 구하려고 줄섰다

articles.title: DOJ Steps Up Agriculture Market Scrutiny Amid Rising Prices
articles.summary: As millions of Americans face an affordability crisis, the Justice Department is homing in on the agriculture industry, focusing on the rising costs faced by farmers and ranchers and soaring prices of key grocery staples like beef and eggs.

articles.title: Cannabis CEO Calls for Reform
articles.summary: Ben Kovler, the Green Thumb Industries Founder and CEO joins Bloomberg Open Interest to break down why federal rescheduling of cannabis could transform the $30B industry overnight. From blocked access to capital markets to booming consumer demand, he explains how outdated regulations are slowing gro

articles.title: Braskem Majority Stake Sale by Novonor Advances With Signed Deal
articles.summary: The sale of a controlling stake in Brazilian petrochemicals producer Braskem SA moved forward another step after troubled conglomerate Novonor signed an agreement to sell its shares to a fund backed by asset manager IG4 Capital.

articles.title: Secondaries Market Goes Mainstream
articles.summary: Private market secondaries had a record 2025. Todd Miller of Jefferies joined Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why this once niche market is now core to institutional investing, how liquidity demand is fueling record growth, and why older private equity assets are still trading at steep discounts.

articles.title: Bank of Canada Surveys Show War Hitting Inflation Expectations
articles.summary: The war in Iran is pushing up inflation expectations, after sentiment had been improving prior to the conflict, survey data released Monday by the Bank of Canada shows.

articles.title: SEC, CFTC Propose Narrowing Hedge Fund Reporting Requirements
articles.summary: Wall Street’s top regulators unveiled a plan to reduce hedge fund reporting requirements, according to a statement.

articles.title: US Stocks Drop as Iran’s Hesitancy on Peace Talks Force Pullback
articles.summary: US stocks fell on Monday as weekend developments in the Middle East war dimmed prospects for peace talks between the US and Iran.

articles.title: Eurizon’s Jen Sees Chinese Yuan Rallying by Record 9% This Year
articles.summary: China may permit record yuan appreciation this year as it tries to boost confidence in its currency and enhance local companies’ purchasing power overseas, according to Stephen Jen, chief investment officer at Eurizon SLJ Capital.

articles.title: Magyar Unveils Cabinet Team to Lead Hungary’s Economic Reset
articles.summary: Hungary’s incoming Prime Minister Peter Magyar named the key ministers for his new government who will help shoulder the task of repairing frayed European ties and relaunching a stagnant economy.

articles.title: Italy Eyes Deficit Under 3% of GDP Even as Iran War Slows Growth
articles.summary: Italy’s government expects the country’s budget deficit to fall below the European Union limit this year despite a downward revision of its growth forecast due to the Iran war, according to people familiar with the matter.

articles.title: Fitch Sees Mozambique Restructure Its Debt Before New IMF Deal
articles.summary: Fitch Ratings sees the possibility of a restructuring of Mozambique’s debt likely ahead of any new deal being secured with the International Monetary Fund.

articles.title: Cleveland-Cliffs ‘No Longer in a Hurry’ on POSCO Deal, CEO Says
articles.summary: Cleveland-Cliffs Inc.’s chief executive officer said improving US steel prices and stronger demand from the American auto industry are taking the pressure off closing a deal with South Korea’s POSCO Holdings Inc.

articles.title: BNP Paribas’ Bigos: Growth Drivers vs. Volatility

articles.title: Iran Hesitant on Talks Even as Trump Says Vance Is on Way
articles.summary: Iran held back from saying whether it would take part in further peace talks even after US President Donald Trump said a delegation is on its way to Pakistan, creating uncertainty about whether a breakthrough can be achieved.

articles.title: Colombia Kicks Off Global Bond Buyback Ahead of Elections
articles.summary: Colombia is buying back some of its outstanding global bonds, its third such operation in the past year, as part of a broader strategy to reduce borrowing costs weeks ahead of a tight presidential race.

articles.title: Treasury Yields Rise Amid Inflation Concerns: Deepali Bhargava
articles.summary: Deepali Bhargava, Head of Research and Chief APAC Economist at ING, highlights recent upward movement in U.S. Treasury yields across two, ten, and thirty-year maturities, driven by concerns over higher inflation and slower economic growth. Despite geopolitical tensions, the Treasury market appears s

articles.title: EQB Inc. Looks to Sell C$200M Limited Recourse Capital Notes
articles.summary: Canada’s EQB Inc., a bank holding company, is preparing to sell around C$200 million ($146 million) of additional tier one securities as soon as Monday, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

articles.title: Strategy Buys $2.54 Billion of Bitcoin, Most Since Late 2024
articles.summary: Michael Saylor’s Strategy Inc. bought $2.54 billion in Bitcoin over the previous seven days, marking the digital asset treasury company’s largest acquisition of the original cryptocurrency since November 2024.

articles.title: European Oil Refiners See Record Weekly Gain for Gasoline Margin
articles.summary: Gasoline’s premium to crude posted its biggest weekly gain on record, a boost for refiners that have been under pressure from high oil prices stoked by the Iran war.

articles.title: Iran Takes Control of Hormuz Again Sending Oil Higher
articles.summary: After some optimism on Friday, Iran took control of the Strait of Hormuz again over the weekend. The US also seized a ship and Tehran fired on vessels in the area deepening the global energy crisis. Bloomberg's Julian Lee has the latest.  (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Agnico Pushes Into Finland With C$3.7 Billion Trio of Gold Deals
articles.summary: Agnico Eagle Mines Ltd. plans to spend C$3.7 billion ($2.7 billion) to acquire three gold projects in northern Finland, expanding the Canadian miner’s footprint in the Scandinavian country.

articles.title: Psychedelic Stocks Soar as Trump Signs Order to Ease Access
articles.summary: Shares of companies involved in psychedelic-tied therapies are surging in Monday trading after President Donald Trump signed an executive order to expedite research and access.

articles.title: ECB’s Pereira Says Economic Damage of Iran War Has Yet to Show
articles.summary: The Iran war is still too fresh to grasp its full impact on the euro-zone economy, according to European Central Bank Governing Council member Alvaro Santos Pereira.

articles.title: India’s RBI Eases Some Curbs on Banks’ Forex Market Trades
articles.summary: India’s central bank eased some restrictions on lenders’ currency market transactions, signaling a partial rollback in some of its most aggressive steps in decades to defend the rupee.

articles.title: Wall Street Strategists Bullish on the US Earnings Outlook
articles.summary: Strategists at some of Wall Street’s biggest banks are upbeat on the outlook for US earnings after a positive start to the first-quarter reporting season.

articles.title: Broker Groww’s Profit Tops Estimates After 100% Post-IPO Surge
articles.summary: Billionbrains Garage Ventures Ltd. reported fourth-quarter profit that was above estimates, as investors seek growth to justify a 100% jump in the stock since its initial public offering — a surge that has made it the world’s most expensive brokerage.

articles.title: Honeywell to Sell Productivity Business in $1.4 Billion Deal
articles.summary: Honeywell International Inc. agreed to divest its productivity solutions and services business to industrial manufacturer Brady Corp., the latest move by Honeywell to reshape its portfolio as it pursues a multipart breakup.

articles.title: Chaos in Hormuz Should Be a Reality Check for Oil Prices
articles.summary: Commodity flows from the Persian Gulf are back at a near-standstill, a reminder of the intractable nature of the war in Iran.

articles.title: Stocks Fall, Oil Rises After US Seizes Iran-Flagged Ship | Bloomberg Brief 4/20/2026
articles.summary: US equity futures fall and oil climbs after the US Navy attacked and boarded an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf of Oman. Iran says it has no plans to attend potential negotiations with the US in Pakistan with the ceasefire expiring on Tuesday. Amy Wu Silverman of RBC Capital Markets discusses

articles.title: Energy Services Set to Benefit Once Iran War Ends, Barclays Says
articles.summary: The European energy services sector faces a brighter future once the Middle East conflict de-escalates as a result of work that oil producers need to undertake to resolve disruption, according to Barclays Plc analysts.

articles.title: Markets Still Have a Lot of Hope and Optimism, Says NewEdge’s Dawson
articles.summary: Cameron Dawson, CIO at NewEdge Wealth, explains what there is to still like about markets and sees earnings growth as idiosyncratic and not broad based. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: UnitedHealth Isn’t Out of the Woods With $6 Billion Medicare Hit
articles.summary: UnitedHealth Group Inc.’s results Tuesday will reflect the full effect of Medicare payment changes set in motion three years ago that cost the company billions, cratering profits last year and blindsiding investors.

articles.title: USA Rare Earth to Buy Brazil’s Serra Verde in $2.8 Billion Deal
articles.summary: USA Rare Earth Inc. agreed to acquire Brazil’s Serra Verde Group in a cash-and-stock transaction, adding to a string of recent deals in the industry.

articles.title: German Banks Aren’t Panicking Over Mythos AI Threat, Sewing Says
articles.summary: Germany’s banks are well-prepared for heightened cyber risks as Anthropic’s new artificial intelligence model sparks global fears of a new era for computer hacks, said Deutsche Bank AG Chief Executive Officer Christian Sewing.

articles.title: Philippines Outlook Cut to Negative by Fitch on Growth Risks
articles.summary: Fitch Ratings revised its outlook on the Philippines’ credit rating to negative from stable, saying the decline in public investment has created risks to economic growth.

articles.title: Finnish Defense Technology Duo Are Said to Weigh Helsinki IPOs
articles.summary: Two Finnish defense technology providers are putting together plans to go public, looking to tap equity markets as Europe ramps up military spending.

articles.title: Drop in UK Bond Sales to Limit Political Risk Selloff, Citi Says
articles.summary: A drop in the UK’s planned bond sales may give the debt some support to counter a selloff driven by political risks, according to Citigroup Inc.

articles.title: Pernod Ricard Is Said to Begin IPO Process for India Unit
articles.summary: Pernod Ricard SA, the maker of Absolut vodka and Chivas Regal whisky, has begun work on a potential initial public offering of its Indian business, according to people familiar with the matter.

articles.title: UAE, US Discuss a Potential Financial Lifeline: WSJ
articles.summary: The United Arab Emirates has reportedly opened talks with the US about a financial backstop in case the country plunges into further crisis due to the war on Iran, according to the Wall Street Journal. Kasia Klimasinska reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Africa’s Oil Shock Was Preventable
articles.summary: Africa’s oil wealth hasn’t translated into fuel security, leaving the continent exposed to shocks like the Iran war, explains Bloomberg Opinion columnist Justice Malala. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Bond Bulls Look to Warsh for Next Leg of Rally
articles.summary: Dovish comments could fuel another leg down in Treasury yields.

articles.title: Singapore Firms Feel Energy Cost Squeeze, Most Hold Off Job Cuts
articles.summary: Singapore companies are exploring adjustments as rising energy prices begin to bite and concerns over labor costs mount.

articles.title: Treasuries Slip as Renewed Ceasefire Tensions Lift Oil Prices
articles.summary: Treasuries retreated slightly on Monday as the US seizure of an Iranian ship dampened hopes of de-escalation in the Middle East conflict and sent oil prices higher.

articles.title: China’s SF Holding Seeks Up to $1 Billion From Convertible Bonds
articles.summary: SF Holding Co., China’s biggest express-delivery firm, is considering raising as much as about $1 billion via a convertible bond offering in Hong Kong that may come alongside a follow-on share placement, according to people familiar with the matter.

articles.title: Iran War: US Seizes Iranian Ship, Casting Doubt on Peace Talks | The Opening Trade 04/20/2026
articles.summary:
Oil jumped, pushing US stocks and Treasuries lower after a turbulent weekend in the Middle East cast doubt on the prospects for peace talks ahead of a looming ceasefire deadline.

Brent rose above $95 a barrel as the US Navy carried out its first seizure of an Iranian vessel in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Emerging-Market Stocks Erase War Losses on AI Demand, Peace Hope
articles.summary: Emerging-market stocks recouped losses since the start of the Iran war, lifted by Asian equities amid optimism about artificial intelligence and lingering hopes for a reduction in Middle East tensions.
